message:
perf(providers): replace .clone() with std::mem::take() for AssistantMessage at stream end

Every provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, OpenAI Responses, Gemini, Cohere, Azure,
Vertex) was deep-cloning the partially-accumulated AssistantMessage when
emitting the final StreamEvent::Done or StreamEvent::Error. Since the partial
message is never used again after the terminal event, this clone was pure
waste — especially expensive for long conversations with large content
vectors and multiple tool-call blocks.

This commit:
- Adds `#[derive(Default)]` to AssistantMessage (src/model.rs) so that
  `std::mem::take()` can swap in a zero-cost default, yielding ownership
  of the accumulated message without any heap allocation or deep copy.
- Replaces every `state.partial.clone()` / `self.partial.clone()` at
  stream-end sites across all 7 provider implementations with
  `std::mem::take(&mut state.partial)` / `std::mem::take(&mut self.partial)`.
- Applies the same optimization in test helpers within azure.rs, gemini.rs,
  openai.rs, and vertex.rs where test streaming loops cloned the partial
  for assertions.

Net effect: eliminates O(n) deep clones of the full message content vector
at stream termination, reducing allocation pressure and latency on long
streaming responses.

Affected providers: Anthropic, Azure, Cohere, Gemini, OpenAI (Chat
Completions), OpenAI (Responses), Vertex.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
